articles.title: CANVA GETS TO $4B IN REVENUE AS LLM REFERRAL TRAFFIC RISES
articles.summary: Canva closed 2025 with $4 billion in annual recurring revenue, over 265 million monthly active users, and a 20% increase in MAUs driven partly by AI tool adoption. The company is pivoting from a design platform with AI features to an "AI platform with design tools," while integrating with chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude. LLM referral traffic is now growing in double-digit percentages, and Canva is actively investing in surfacing its content through AI-powered search.

articles.title: THIS NEW AI ‘EYEDROPPER' TOOL BRINGS ONE OF THE MOST POWERFUL UX TRICKS INTO THE AI AGE
articles.summary: Variant's new AI eyedropper feature lets users click on one generated interface to transfer its style onto another. However, the tool's usefulness is limited because Variant's AI engine produces designs that already look too similar, and it can't sample from external sources like bitmaps or Figma. The feature nonetheless points toward a promising direction: giving designers direct manipulation tools for AI instead of relying on imprecise text prompts.

articles.title: NOTHING'S LATEST STUNT OUTRAGES APPLE FANS
articles.summary: Ahead of Apple's 4 March event, Nothing cheekily hijacked the hype by spray-painting “Nothing” and “5th March” over Apple's invite, likely teasing the Nothing Phone (4a) Pro launch a day later. While some fans praised the bold, counter-culture stunt, others slammed it as unprofessional, though the move reinforces Nothing's provocative brand identity and willingness to challenge Apple's dominance.

articles.title: RITE TESTING: HOW TO TEST AI-DRIVEN PRODUCTS IN A MEANINGFUL WAY
articles.summary: RITE (Rapid Iterative Testing and Evaluation) offers a scrappier, cheaper alternative to formalized UX research by testing with smaller groups and iterating prototypes in real time. Traditional user testing has become too expensive and siloed, often producing reports rather than actionable solutions, making it vulnerable to AI-based alternatives. By focusing on co-creating use cases with real users early in the process, RITE testing helps teams build products people actually want to buy.

articles.title: IT'S TIME FOR APPLE TO LET GO OF 60HZ DISPLAYS
articles.summary: Apple has treated 120Hz ProMotion displays as a premium feature for nearly a decade, limiting them mostly to Pro devices due to the smoother experience they provide. Given the large price gaps between mid-range and Pro models, and the fact that Apple's products aren't cheap to begin with, it feels outdated for devices like the iPad Air and MacBook Air to still ship with 60Hz displays instead of at least 90Hz or 120Hz.

articles.title: COGNITIVE SOVEREIGNTY IN THE AGE OF PERSONALISATION ALGORITHMS
articles.summary: Modern personalized systems don't just reduce friction—they increasingly shape what we think about next, optimising for engagement over understanding and quietly eroding “cognitive sovereignty,” the ability to explore and decide independently. The challenge isn't personalization itself, but business models and design defaults that prioritize retention over agency, making it crucial to build systems with transparency, user control, friction where needed, and space for genuine discovery rather than tightly steered attention.

articles.title: GENERATE STUDIO-QUALITY EXPLAINER VIDEOS
articles.summary: Lunair is the fastest way to add a video to your landing page. It's as easy as writing, and users have total creative control.

articles.title: AI DESIGN AGENT FOR PRODUCT TEAMS
articles.summary: Figr is the first design agent that actually understands your product. Drop in context like industry benchmarks, user feedback, product flow, and design systems to get production-ready UX backed by real app patterns.

articles.title: THE NEXT RENAISSANCE: WHY CREATIVITY IS THE CURRENCY OF THE AI AGE
articles.summary: As AI automates repetitive tasks, human creativity, imagination, and the ability to ask better questions are becoming the most valuable professional assets. The World Economic Forum projects 92 million jobs displaced by 2030 but 170 million new roles created, with creative thinking among the fastest-growing skills in demand. Rather than competing with machines, the next decade will reward those who leverage AI for analytical tasks while applying uniquely human qualities like intuition, empathy, and visionary thinking.

articles.title: BUILD SOMETHING SILLY
articles.summary: AI tools now let non-developers build custom software. This author rebuilt a website and created a personal invoicing tool using Cursor and Claude, replacing paid SaaS subscriptions. Wall Street has taken notice too, with SaaS stocks losing $285 billion after Anthropic released new AI tools, signaling a shift in how individuals and small businesses approach software. The key takeaway isn't any specific tool, but the mindset shift from "what should I subscribe to?" to "could I build this myself?" — a muscle best developed by starting small.

articles.title: FILL YOUR OWN CUP WITH WHAT GIVES YOU ENERGY
articles.summary: To balance creativity with performance demands, intentionally protect two or three high-energy time blocks each week for meaningful, focused work—shielding them from meetings and inbox noise—so creativity becomes a structured priority rather than an indulgence, ultimately improving both impact and productivity.

articles.title: FROM APPLE TO YOUTUBE: THESE LOGOS ARE A LESSON IN LONGEVITY
articles.summary: Brands like Twinings, Coca-Cola, Nike, Apple, and YouTube prove that the most powerful logos endure by resisting change rather than chasing trends.

articles.title: HOW EARLY ACCESSIBILITY SOLUTIONS EVOLVED INTO CORE UX DESIGN PRINCIPLES
articles.summary: Many widely used innovations — from typewriters and audiobooks to touchscreens and GPS — originated as accessibility solutions for people with disabilities before gaining mainstream adoption.

articles.title: FROM DOCTOR WHO TO STAR WARS, HOW ARTIST MATTHEW SAVAGE HAS SHAPED STRIKING ON-SCREEN WORLDS
articles.summary: Concept designer Matthew Savage built his career from entry-level film roles to major productions like Alien: Romulus and Star Wars by grounding imaginative sci-fi designs in real-world function, embracing 3D workflows, and developing a distinctive creative voice shaped by broad life experience.
